title_of_original: Commission Decision of 22 November 2006 on State aid N 157/06, United Kingdom South Yorkshire Digital Region Broadband Project
case_description: [1] In January 2025, the Greek Ministry of Digital Governance approved Grant No. 27/2025, [2] authorizing KritiNet S.A. (hereinafter KritiNet) to implement and operate high-speed broadband infrastructure under the "Crete Next-Gen Connectivity Project." [3] The project targets underserved rural and mountainous areas across the island of Crete and [4] forms part of Greece's operational programme for digital development and regional cohesion. [5] The total eligible investment costs of the project were set at EUR 42,000,000. [6] The Greek Ministry of Digital Governance decided that the project would be co-financed by the European Regional Development Fund (ERDF). [6] The ERDF contributes EUR 30,000,000, [7] while the remaining EUR 12,000,000 is provided from the Greek state budget. [8] The Ministry awarded the funding to KritiNet through direct administrative designation, [9] citing the company's prior local experience. [10] Under the grant terms, KritiNet receives the full amount of public financing as a non-repayable capital grant. [11] The company will own the infrastructure and is authorized to operate it commercially for a minimum period of 10 years. [12] The grant decision does not include a clawback mechanism, [13] nor does it impose binding obligations to provide wholesale access to third-party service providers. [14] Pricing conditions, access terms, and performance benchmarks were not specified in the award decision. [15] No formal monitoring structure or public service obligations were foreseen.
